revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:兵庫縣神戸護國神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| name = Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine | image = File:Gokoku sharine shoumen.JPG | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyogo|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Nada-ku, Kobe|en|Nada-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1154555|lt=Nada-ku|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, [[Shinohara Kitamachi]] 4-5-1 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.721889|N|135.224583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|eirei|qid=Q11617005|1=eirei}} | type = Formerly {{ill|Home Ministry|en|Home Ministry|qid=Q1142265|lt=Home Ministry|1=Home Ministry}} designated {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社#指定護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}
[[Bekkaku Shrine]] | established = 1941 (Shōwa 16) June | main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] | festival = May 6 (Spring Memorial festival)
November 6 (Autumn Memorial festival) | 神事 = Mitama festival (last Friday and Saturday of July) | map = {{location map|Kobe#Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=43|lat_sec=18.8|lon_deg=135|lon_min=13|lon_sec=28.5|label=兵庫縣神戸護國神社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Nada-ku|qid=Q1154555|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}} }} '''Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine''' (兵庫縣神戸護國神社) is a {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|qid=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}} located in {{ill|Nada-ku, Kobe|en|Nada-ku, Kobe|qid=Q1154555|lt=Nada-ku, Kobe|1=Nada-ku, Kobe}}, {{ill|Kobe|en|Kobe|qid=Q48320|lt=Kobe|1=Kobe}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, Japan. It enshrines 53,257 war dead from the eastern part of Hyōgo Prefecture (Tanba, Settsu, and Awaji). The shrine grounds cover 2200 tsubo (about 7260 square meters), and cherry trees have been planted in the grounds and along the road in front of the shrine to console the spirits of the war dead. == Overview == Since the Meiji period, a soul-inviting festival (Shōkonsai) for war dead was held with a combined effort of government and civilians at a temporary festival site in {{ill|Egeyama|ja|会下山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egeyama|1=Egeyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11381183}} (present-day Kaigahama-cho, Hyogo-ku, Kobe). However, due to the desire of various members of the prefecture to build a proper shrine to honor the war dead, it was decided to establish the shrine on the site of the former {{ill|Kwansei Gakuin University|ja|関西学院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kwansei Gakuin University|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 学校法人関西学院|1=Kwansei Gakuin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11448737}} in Oji-cho, Nada-ku, Kobe, covering about 3000 tsubo (about 9917 square meters). The groundbreaking ceremony was held on April 27, 1940 (Shōwa 15), and the main shrine and other shrine buildings were constructed. The shrine was designated a Gokoku Shrine by the Interior Minister on June 25, 1941 (Shōwa 16). The enshrinement festival was held over three days starting July 17, 1941, with a purification ceremony, shrine dedication, and various other ceremonies. The shrine buildings were destroyed in the Kobe Air Raid on June 5, 1945 (Shōwa 20). After World War II, under GHQ (General Headquarters, Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers) occupation, the shrine was renamed "Hyogo Goryo Shrine" to avoid being seen as a militaristic facility. On September 18, 1949 (Shōwa 24), the shrine was relocated to its current site in Shinohara Kitamachi, Nada-ku, Kobe. In 1950 (Shōwa 25), the Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine Supporters' Association was established to oversee the rebuilding of the shrine buildings. After Japan regained sovereignty in 1952 (Shōwa 27), the shrine's name was reverted to "Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine." In January 1957 (Shōwa 32), the administrative office was completed, and on March 17, 1959 (Shōwa 34), the temporary shrine relocation festival was held. The main shrine relocation festival was held on November 27, 1959, followed by a dedication ceremony on November 28. The relocation festival included offerings from Emperor Showa and was attended by many dignitaries. The total construction cost was 50 million yen (at that time). On November 25, 1960 (Shōwa 35), Emperor Showa made an offering to the shrine. Over the years, the shrine grounds and buildings were further developed and renovated. The administrative office was severely damaged in the Great Hanshin Earthquake on January 7, 1995 (Heisei 7), but restoration work was completed in November 1996 (Heisei 8). In 2011 (Heisei 23), the torii was removed due to deterioration, but it was rebuilt in November 2013 (Heisei 25) with donations from bereaved families and supporters. Hyōgo Prefecture also has another Gokoku Shrine, the {{ill|Hyogo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|ja|兵庫縣姫路護國神社|1=Hyōgo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyogo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyogo Himeji Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11393652}} in {{ill|Himeji|en|Himeji|qid=Q209801|lt=Himeji|1=Himeji}}, which enshrines war dead from the western part of the prefecture. == festivals == === Main festivals === * Spring Memorial festival (May 6) * Autumn Memorial festival (November 6) === Other ceremony === * Monthly Virtue festival (18th of every month) * National Foundation Day festival and First Horse festival (February 11) * End-of-War Memorial festival (August 15) === Events === * Cherry Blossom festival (late March to mid-April) * Mitama festival (last Friday and Saturday of July) File:兵庫県神戸護国神社 鳥居.jpg| Former torii (removed in 2011) File:兵庫県神戸護国神社 拝殿.jpg| Worship hall (viewed from the southwest) File:Kobe gokoku ireihi1.JPG| Memorial monument for the 14th Air Brigade headquarters and the 68th and 70th Air Squadrons File:Kobe gokoku ireihi 2.JPG| Memorial monument for the northern non-native people who perished in the war File:Kobe gokoku itrihi 3.JPG| Memorial tower for the fallen soldiers of the Echo 5333 unit File:Keidaijinzya.JPG| Suehiro Inari Shrine (on the shrine grounds) File:Norinomiyasama tanzyou.JPG| Commemorative tree plaque for the birth of Princess Aiko == Access == * JR Rokko Station / Hankyu Rokko Station, Kobe City Bus No. 2, get off at Gokoku Shrine bus stop * From Sannomiya Station (subway), Kobe City Bus No. 2 towards Hankyu Rokko / JR Rokko, get off at Gokoku Shrine bus stop == Surroundings == * About 160 meters south-southeast of Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine is the headquarters of the largest yakuza organization, the Yamaguchi-gumi, located in Shinohara Honmachi 4-3-1, Nada-ku, Kobe. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine History * {{Cite book |和書 |editor=兵庫県神社庁 |title=神祇史年表 |volume=下卷 |edition=増補版 |publisher=明文社 |date=1968-12-01 |doi=10.11501/3451657}} == External Links == * [https://www.hyogo-jinjacho.com/data/6301024.html Hyōgo Prefecture Kobe Gokoku Shrine] - Hyōgo Prefecture Shrine Office {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|兵庫縣神戸護國神社|version=102008359|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:兵庫縣神戸護國神社]] {{Commons category|Hyogoken-Kobe-gokoku-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11393653}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Hyogo Kobe Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
